title: To His Honoured Friend, Sir John Mince.
content:
For civil, clean, and circumcised wit,
    And for the comely carriage of it,
    Thou art the man, the only man best known,
    Mark'd for the true wit of a million:
    From whom we'll reckon. Wit came in but since
    The calculation of thy birth, brave Mince.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To His Honoured Friend, Sir John Mince.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
